title: Antifragile: Things That Gain from Disorder
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
authorBio: Nassim Nicholas Taleb (born September 12, 1960, in Amioun, Lebanon) is a Lebanese-American essayist, mathematical statistician, former derivatives trader, risk analyst, and aphorist. He spent 21 years as a quantitative trader on Wall Street before shifting to full-time scholarship. His work concerns problems of randomness, probability, complexity, and uncertainty. He holds an MBA from the Wharton School and a PhD in management science from the University of Paris (Dauphine). He is Distinguished Professor of Risk Engineering at NYU Tandon School of Engineering. His five-volume Incerto series—Fooled by Randomness, The Black Swan, The Bed of Procrustes, Antifragile, and Skin in the Game—is a philosophical essay on uncertainty that has been translated into 49 languages. The Sunday Times named The Black Swan one of the 12 most influential books since World War II.
